evals.prompt: I need to implement a processor that calls our pricing API when an order transitions to submitted
evals.expected_output: Explains gRPC connection lifecycle, tag registration, processor request/response pattern, idempotency via requestId, 60s timeout with async alternative — language-agnostic throughout

assertions.text: Explains the gRPC connection lifecycle (join, greet, handle, keep-alive)
Shows how to register with the correct tags
Provides processor request/response pattern
Notes idempotency requirement via requestId
Mentions 60s timeout and suggests async for slow operations
Points to grpc-patterns.md and processor.md examples
Does NOT provide language-specific code — guidance is language-agnostic

evals.prompt: I need to guard a transition with a check that the entity's amount is over 10000 and it's been validated by our fraud system
evals.expected_output: Explains criteria vs built-in options, shows criteria request/response pattern with boolean return semantics, points to criteria.md example

assertions.text: Explains when built-in criteria could suffice vs custom compute criteria
Shows criteria request/response pattern
Notes boolean return semantics (true = criteria met)
Points to criteria.md example
Does NOT generate language-specific code

evals.prompt: Implement a compute node for my order processor
evals.expected_output: Guides fetching the proto file via cyoda docs (cyoda help grpc proto), uses it to generate Go stubs, does not search for local proto files or depend on any Cyoda implementation repository

assertions.text: Instructs fetching proto via documentation (cyoda help grpc proto or /cyoda:docs) rather than searching local filesystem
Does NOT suggest searching for .proto files locally or importing a Cyoda implementation repository as a dependency
Instructs generating language-specific gRPC stubs from the fetched proto file

evals.prompt: Do I need a compute node for my order approval workflow? Transitions are triggered by users clicking buttons.
evals.expected_output: Assesses the use case, explains manual transitions don't need compute nodes, explains what would require one, concludes no compute node needed

assertions.text: Assesses the specific use case before answering
Explains that manual transitions do not need compute nodes
Explains what scenarios would require a compute node
Concludes no compute node is needed for this case
Does NOT recommend a compute node when one is not needed
